skill.name: engineering-fundamentals
skill.description: Provides a practical code review checklist for naming, function design, organization, comments, SOLID principles, and maintainability coaching.
skill.summary: Review code for maintainability, naming, function design, organization, and SOLID fundamentals.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks as command execution and text substrings as weak cryptography. Manual review found only review guidance, examples, and checklists in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, shell invocation, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection evidence was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to inline markdown code, fenced code examples, a table of naming conventions, and a documentation path. These examples are review material and are not executable shell or Ruby backtick expressions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched locations are prose, tables, inline code, or fenced examples in SKILL.md. I found no command runner, script, or instruction that would execute those snippets.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Substring Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts correspond to ordinary words and headings such as description, Descriptive, and Function Design. SKILL.md contains no cryptographic implementation, dependency, API call, or recommendation to use weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is natural language review guidance, not cryptographic code. No evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or related weak algorithm usage was found.

content.value_statement: Code reviews can miss basic maintainability issues. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a clear checklist for naming, function design, organization, comments, and SOLID review.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether names are descriptive, specific, and consistent with common conventions.
Reviews function size, responsibility, parameter count, side effects, and nesting.
Flags duplication, weak cohesion, unclear separation, and premature abstraction.
Guides comment and documentation review for public APIs and dead code.
Applies a quick SOLID principles checklist to classes, functions, and interfaces.
Provides Socratic questions for mentoring during code review.
limitations: It is a review checklist and does not run tests, linters, or static analysis tools.
It gives general engineering guidance, not language-specific style rules.
It cannot verify runtime behavior unless the user provides enough code context.
The referenced standards path may not exist in every workspace.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request for Maintainability
use_cases.description: Apply a structured checklist to find unclear naming, large functions, duplication, and weak organization before merge.

use_cases.title: Coach Junior Developers
use_cases.description: Turn direct feedback into focused questions that help developers identify readability and design issues themselves.

use_cases.title: Plan a Small Refactor
use_cases.description: Identify where to extract functions, rename variables, reduce nesting, and remove accidental complexity.

prompt_templates.title: Review Basic Readability
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change for engineering fundamentals. Focus on naming, function size, readability, and comments. Give concise feedback with examples.

prompt_templates.title: Find Refactoring Opportunities
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this code for duplication, deep nesting, large functions, and premature abstraction. Recommend focused refactors with priority and rationale.

prompt_templates.title: Mentor Through Questions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request as a mentor. Use Socratic questions for naming, function design, duplication, and abstraction choices.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Design Fundamentals
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this module against SOLID principles, cohesion, separation of concerns, side effects, and maintainability risks. Provide an ordered review summary.

output_examples.input: A pull request with several long functions and unclear variable names.
output: The review identifies names that need more context and functions that combine validation, persistence, and notification work.
It recommends extracting responsibilities, using early returns, and adding documentation only where public behavior needs explanation.

output_examples.input: A junior developer asks how to improve a service module.
output: The response asks focused questions about responsibility, duplication, and future change points.
It highlights one small refactor at a time instead of giving a broad rewrite request.

output_examples.input: A team wants to check whether an abstraction is justified.
output_examples.output: The review compares current reuse, configuration complexity, and expected change frequency before recommending whether to keep or remove the abstraction.

best_practices: Provide the code, surrounding context, and the goal of the review.
Ask for prioritized findings so the most important changes are handled first.
Use the Socratic questions when the review is intended for mentoring.
anti_patterns: Using the checklist as a replacement for tests, linters, or security review.
Requesting broad rewrites when a small naming or extraction change is enough.
Applying every guideline mechanically without considering the code context.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews naming, function design, organization, comments, SOLID principles, and common maintainability red flags.

faq.question: Does it execute code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is markdown guidance and does not include scripts, tools, or executable commands.

faq.question: Can it replace a linter?
faq.answer: No. It complements linters by reviewing readability, design intent, and maintainability choices.

faq.question: Is it useful for mentoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Socratic questions for helping developers reason about code quality.

faq.question: Which languages does it support?
faq.answer: The guidance is language-neutral and works best for general application code reviews.

faq.question: What input should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the code change, review goals, relevant constraints, and any team conventions that should be respected.
